front.title: Pre-I-D Profile: PQ-Hybrid Anonymous Authorization over Privacy Pass and MOQT

abstract.t: This document is a pre-Internet-Draft work-in-progress profile skeleton. It records a separation between a classical outer MOQT admission plane and a future end-to-end PQ anonymous authorization/key-release plane. It does not select a post-quantum anonymous primitive, request a codepoint, define a Privacy Pass token type, or imply IETF endorsement or adoption.

note.title: Pre-Submission Status
note.t: This RFCXML source has not been submitted. Its generated artifacts contain standard Internet-Draft boilerplate solely to exercise the pre-submission toolchain; that boilerplate is not evidence of submission, IETF endorsement, adoption, working-group status, or codepoint allocation. The profile is intentionally incomplete pending construction selection and independent cryptographic and privacy review.

section.name: Introduction
section.t: This skeleton applies the architecture of , the HTTP scheme of where HTTP is used, and the classical issuance protocols in as references. Those documents do not select the PQ anonymous primitive required for a PqHybrid authorization claim. The MOQT carriage draft () and MoQ Privacy Pass authentication draft () are work in progress. The individual token-binding draft () is not adopted. Hybrid terminology follows and the signature-composition taxonomy in .

section.name: Requirements Language
section.t: The key words MUST and MUST NOT in this document are to be interpreted as described in BCP 14 when, and only when, they appear in all capitals, as shown here.

dl.dd: Classical, carrier-local relay admission, routing, rate control, or cache policy using standard MOQT/Privacy Pass surfaces.
End-to-end encrypted ordinary application Object material evaluated by the HyprStream origin.
A future typed, verified authorization result, distinct from unauthenticated and identified principals.
Release of a sealed unary response, stream traffic package, or group epoch package to the exact bound recipient.
An unmodified classical MOQT relay that only forwards or caches ordinary Object bytes.

section.name: Assurance Separation
section.t: The outer admission result MUST NOT be accepted as inner authorization, MAC clearance, or authority to release plaintext or an epoch key. The origin MUST verify an inner capability and its exact binding before dispatch or key release. A relay MUST NOT be given or recover inner plaintext, decoded anonymous-capability or MAC-clearance material, a response key, a traffic key, or a group epoch key. Forwarding an opaque encrypted Object payload does not give the relay the enclosed plaintext material.

The future inner construction is not specified by this document. A classical entitlement or a hybrid signature wrapper is not by itself anonymous issuance and cannot raise an authorization result to PqHybrid. Issuer, origin, controller, and recipient keys are purpose-separated and selected from accepted-current state.

section.name: Classical MOQT Relay Profile
section.t: Clients MUST send ordinary MOQT Object payload bytes that a stock relay can forward and cache unchanged. The profile MUST NOT require a MOQT extension or codepoint for the relay to forward the Object. A participating relay may use standard authorization-token surfaces for coarse admission; an unsupported private authorization profile must not make forwarding non-interoperable.

Private challenge, issuance, redemption, and key-release data are future encrypted application Object payloads. The relay-visible Object identity, size, timing, cache behavior, and routing metadata are not confidential by this document and no traffic-analysis resistance is claimed.

section.name: Transcript Registry
section.t: The local registry in domain-separation.json names labels, field bounds, canonicalization, and owners. Implementations MUST reject an unknown registry label, a non-canonical encoding, or an out-of-bounds transcript field before cryptographic processing. An implementation MUST NOT substitute a holder DID, ATProto handle, or relay route for an inner binding field.
The registry is local to this repository. It is not an IANA registry or a request to allocate Privacy Pass, MOQT, or any other codepoint.

section.name: Construction Status and Experimental Refusal Boundary
section.t: A 2026-07-17 primary-source audit found promising post-quantum blind-signature and anonymous-credential research, but no candidate supplied the complete reviewed Privacy Pass construction, fixed parameters, maintained implementation, canonical profile encoding and vectors, and analysis of this exact classical-plus-PQ composition. A production implementation MUST NOT issue, accept, redeem, clear a MAC, generate an authorization result, dispatch, or release a key under this profile while the PQ construction and token type are unselected. The experimental boundary checker MUST terminate a structurally valid fixture with the ConstructionUnselected refusal.

The repository-local experimental boundary is a deterministic non-cryptographic fixture schema, not a token type or protocol encoding. The experimental boundary checker MUST require exactly two ordered roles, ClassicalAnonymous then PqAnonymous, combined with AND, and MUST reject a missing, duplicated, reordered, unknown, crossed, or stripped leg. The boundary MUST reject any holder DID, ATProto handle, account identifier, or other stable holder subject field. Each leg MUST use its dedicated Privacy Pass issuer-key purpose, and the boundary MUST reject cross-role key reuse or substitution of a mesh, PDS, response, or controller key. The boundary MUST require exact accepted-current issuer, audience, state epoch and digest, policy generation, expiry, redemption context, and resource-profile commitment values and MUST reject stale, rolled-back, expired, or crossed values. The experimental fixture boundary MUST derive its challenge digest from the canonical repository fixture challenge and MUST require the evaluator-authoritative context digest in every leg of every message.

A structurally valid experimental fixture still terminates in the ConstructionUnselected refusal. It is not a positive cryptographic vector and cannot mint PqHybrid assurance. Standard classical Privacy Pass remains available only for its standard classical purpose; standard MOQT relays remain unchanged and forward ordinary opaque application Object bytes.

section.name: State-Machine Skeleton
section.t: Client states are: Idle; EntitlementChecked (optional); IssuancePrepared; TokenHeld; OuterSessionReady (optional); InnerControlSent; Released; SpentOrExpired; Denied. Issuer states are: ConfigurationResolved; IssuanceReceived; IssuanceAccepted; IssuanceRejected; KeyDraining. Origin states are: ChallengeIssued; OuterSessionObserved (optional); InnerControlReceived; StateResolved; BindingVerified; MACAuthorized; SpendCommitted; ReleaseCommitted; Denied. No transition to dispatch or release is defined from an outer-only state.

Construction selection must define persistence, atomic spend/commit, retry, expiry, accepted-state advance, key rotation, crash recovery, and error behavior. Spend is committed only after all authorization checks and before dispatch or key release. A crash after spend commit may burn the one-use capability but cannot repeat authorization; a crash before spend commit cannot create an authorization success.

The bounded inner control kinds are Challenge, IssuanceRequest, IssuanceResponse, Redemption, AuthorizationResult, KeyReleaseRequest, KeyReleaseResponse, and Error. The local scaffold limits one encrypted Object payload to 65536 bytes, one exchange to 16 Objects, and one session to 8 concurrent exchanges. These names and ceilings are application-profile inputs, not MOQT or Privacy Pass allocations; unknown kinds and versions reject.

section.name: Security Considerations
section.t: Construction selection and independent review are prerequisites for any PqHybrid production claim. The profile requires exact binding to issuer, audience, accepted-state and policy generations, carrier profile, operation/resource, one-use PoP, and HyKEM recipient, but deliberately leaves their concrete encoding and primitive unselected.

section.name: Privacy Considerations
section.t: The profile makes no issuance/redemption unlinkability claim against issuer-origin collusion, no traffic-analysis claim against an origin-relay or global observer, and no protection against PDS, ledger, storage, or cross-cell correlation beyond stated data minimization. See the companion privacy and threat analyses for the required collusion model.

section.name: IANA Considerations
section.t: This document requests no IANA actions and allocates no codepoints.

front.title: Key words for use in RFCs to Indicate Requirement Levels
Ambiguity of Uppercase vs Lowercase in RFC 2119 Key Words
The Privacy Pass HTTP Authentication Scheme
Terminology for Post-Quantum Traditional Hybrid Schemes
Privacy Pass Authentication for Media over QUIC (MoQ)
Privacy Pass with Token Binding Extension
